testcase.name: test_authenticate

failure.message: AttributeError: module 'urllib3' has no attribute 'Request'. Did you mean: 'request'?

testcase.failure:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4414124256'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4414124592'> mock_request = <MagicMock name='request' id='4414124928'>
@mock.patch("urllib3.PoolManager.request") @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth") @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_authenticate(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_request): auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__")
mock_cognito.assert_called_once() assert mock_cognito.call_args.kwargs["username"] == "__username__"
mock_srp_auth.assert_called_once_with( password="__password__", cognito=mock_cognito.return_value )
> auth.authenticate()
tests/test_auth.py:26: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ pyschlage/auth.py:41: in wrapper return fn(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <pyschlage.auth.Auth object at 0x1071a34d0>
@_translate_auth_errors def authenticate(self): """Performs authentication with AWS.
:raise pyschlage.exceptions.NotAuthorizedError: When authentication fails. :raise pyschlage.exceptions.UnknownError: On other errors. """ > self.auth(urllib3.Request()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E AttributeError: module 'urllib3' has no attribute 'Request'. Did you mean: 'request'?
pyschlage/auth.py:103: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_request

failure.message: TypeError: '>=' not supported between instances of 'MagicMock' and 'int'

testcase.name: test_request_not_authorized

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: request('GET', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', timeout=60, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, fields={'baz': 'bam'}) Actual: request('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', baz='bam', auth=<MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4414951104'>, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, timeout=60)

testcase.failure:
TypeError: missing a required argument: 'url'
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4414950768'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4414950432'> mock_request = <MagicMock name='request' spec='function' id='4414951440'>
@mock.patch("urllib3.PoolManager.request", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_request_not_authorized(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_request): url = "https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar" auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_request.side_effect = ClientError( { "Error": { "Code": "NotAuthorizedException", "Message": f"Unauthorized for url: {url}", } }, "foo-op", )
with pytest.raises( pyschlage.exceptions.NotAuthorizedError, match=f"Unauthorized for url: {url}" ): auth.request("get", "/foo/bar", baz="bam")
> mock_request.assert_called_once_with( "GET", url, timeout=60, headers={"X-Api-Key": _auth.API_KEY}, fields={"baz": "bam"}, )
tests/test_auth.py:66: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ /opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/unittest/mock.py:997: in assert_called_once_with return self.assert_called_with(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='request' spec='function' id='4414951440'> args = ('GET', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar') kwargs = {'fields': {'baz': 'bam'}, 'headers': {'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, 'timeout': 60} expected = TypeError("missing a required argument: 'url'") actual = TypeError("missing a required argument: 'url'") _error_message = <function NonCallableMock.assert_called_with.<locals>._error_message at 0x107186cf0> cause = TypeError("missing a required argument: 'url'")
def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\n Actual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found. E Expected: request('GET', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', timeout=60, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, fields={'baz': 'bam'}) E Actual: request('get', 'https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar', baz='bam', auth=<MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth()' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4414951104'>, headers={'X-Api-Key': 'hnuu9jbbJr7MssFDWm5nU2Z7nG5Q5rxsaqWsE7e9'}, timeout=60)
/opt/anaconda3/envs/SWA_Rep2/lib/python3.14/unittest/mock.py:985: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_request_unknown_error

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. testcase.failure:
TypeError: missing a required argument: 'url'
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
mock_cognito = <MagicMock name='Cognito' id='4414952112'> mock_srp_auth = <MagicMock name='RequestsSrpAuth' spec='RequestsSrpAuth' id='4414953120'> mock_request = <MagicMock name='request' spec='function' id='4414953792'>
@mock.patch("urllib3.PoolManager.request", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.utils.RequestsSrpAuth", spec=True) @mock.patch("pycognito.Cognito") def test_request_unknown_error(mock_cognito, mock_srp_auth, mock_request): url = "https://api.allegion.yonomi.cloud/v1/foo/bar" auth = _auth.Auth("__username__", "__password__") mock_resp = mock.create_autospec(urllib3.response.HTTPResponse) mock_resp.status = 500 mock_resp.data = b'{"error": "Internal"}' mock_request.return_value = mock_resp
with pytest.raises(pyschlage.exceptions.UnknownError): auth.request("get", "/foo/bar", baz="bam")
> mock_request.assert_called_once_with( "GET", url, timeout=60, headers={"X-Api-Key": _auth.API_KEY}, fields={"baz": "bam"}, )

testcase.name: test_to_from_json_temporary_schedule

failure.message:
AssertionError: assert AccessCode(na...isabled=False) == AccessCode(na...isabled=False)
Omitting 10 identical items, use -vv to show Differing attributes: ['schedule']
Drill down into differing attribute schedule: schedule: TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 12, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 13, 0)) != TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0))...
...Full output truncated (9 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_code.TestAccessCode object at 0x1070e56e0> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4414115856'> access_code_json = {'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'accesscodeId': '__access_code_uuid__', 'activationSecs': 0, ...} wifi_device = Device(device_id='__wifi_uuid__', device_type='be489wifi')
def test_to_from_json_temporary_schedule( self, mock_auth: Mock, access_code_json: dict[str, Any], wifi_device: Device ): access_code_id = "__access_code_uuid__" sched = TemporarySchedule( start=datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30, 0), end=datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0, 0), ) json = deepcopy(access_code_json) json["activationSecs"] = 1671957000 json["expirationSecs"] = 1671958800 code = AccessCode( _auth=mock_auth, _json=json, _device=wifi_device, name="Friendly name", code="0123", schedule=sched, device_id=wifi_device.device_id, access_code_id=access_code_id, ) > assert AccessCode.from_json(mock_auth, wifi_device, json) == code E AssertionError: assert AccessCode(na...isabled=False) == AccessCode(na...isabled=False) E E Omitting 10 identical items, use -vv to show E Differing attributes: E ['schedule'] E E Drill down into differing attribute schedule: E schedule: TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 12, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 13, 0)) != TemporarySchedule(start=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 8, 30), end=datetime.datetime(2022, 12, 25, 9, 0))... E E ...Full output truncated (9 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show
tests/test_code.py:75: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_lock_ble

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: request('post', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands', json={'data': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'deviceId': '__ble_uuid__', 'state': 1, 'userId': '<user-id>'}, 'name': 'changelockstate'}) Actual: request('POST', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands', body='{"data": {"CAT": "abcdef", "deviceId": "__ble_uuid__", "state": 1, "userId": "<user-id>"}, "name": "changelockstate"}', headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_lock.TestLock object at 0x107148910> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4415944560'> ble_lock_json = {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'attributes': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'adminOnlyEnabled': 0, ...}, 'connected': False, ...}
def test_lock_ble(self, mock_auth, ble_lock_json): with pytest.raises(NotAuthenticatedError): Lock().lock()
lock = Lock.from_json(mock_auth, ble_lock_json) lock.lock()
command_json = { "data": { "CAT": "abcdef", "deviceId": "__ble_uuid__", "state": 1, "userId": "<user-id>", }, "name": "changelockstate", } > mock_auth.request.assert_called_once_with( "post", "devices/__ble_uuid__/commands", json=command_json )

self = <MagicMock name='mock.request' spec_set='function' id='4415637552'> args = ('post', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands') kwargs = {'json': {'data': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'deviceId': '__ble_uuid__', 'state': 1, 'userId': '<user-id>'}, 'name': 'changelockstate'}} expected = call('', ('post', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands'), {'json': {'data': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'deviceId': '__ble_uuid__', 'state': 1, 'userId': '<user-id>'}, 'name': 'changelockstate'}}) actual = call('', ('POST', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands'), {'body': '{"data": {"CAT": "abcdef", "deviceId": "__ble_uuid__", "state": 1, "userId": "<user-id>"}, "name": "changelockstate"}', 'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}}) _error_message = <function NonCallableMock.assert_called_with.<locals>._error_message at 0x1073c8460> cause = None
def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.
Raises an AssertionError if the args and keyword args passed in are different to the last call to the mock.""" if self.call_args is None: expected = self._format_mock_call_signature(args, kwargs) actual = 'not called.' error_message = ('expected call not found.\nExpected: %s\n Actual: %s' % (expected, actual)) raise AssertionError(error_message)
def _error_message(): msg = self._format_mock_failure_message(args, kwargs) return msg expected = self._call_matcher(_Call((args, kwargs), two=True)) actual = self._call_matcher(self.call_args) if actual != expected: cause = expected if isinstance(expected, Exception) else None > raise AssertionError(_error_message()) from cause E AssertionError: expected call not found. E Expected: request('post', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands', json={'data': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'deviceId': '__ble_uuid__', 'state': 1, 'userId': '<user-id>'}, 'name': 'changelockstate'}) E Actual: request('POST', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands', body='{"data": {"CAT": "abcdef", "deviceId": "__ble_uuid__", "state": 1, "userId": "<user-id>"}, "name": "changelockstate"}', headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})

testcase.name: test_unlock_ble

failure.message: AssertionError: expected call not found. Expected: request('post', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands', json={'data': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'deviceId': '__ble_uuid__', 'state': 0, 'userId': '<user-id>'}, 'name': 'changelockstate'}) Actual: request('POST', 'devices/__ble_uuid__/commands', body='{"data": {"CAT": "abcdef", "deviceId": "__ble_uuid__", "state": 0, "userId": "<user-id>"}, "name": "changelockstate"}', headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})

self = <tests.test_lock.TestLock object at 0x107148c90> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4415943216'> ble_lock_json = {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'attributes': {'CAT': 'abcdef', 'SAT': 'ghijkl', 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'adminOnlyEnabled': 0, ...}, 'connected': False, ...}
def test_unlock_ble(self, mock_auth, ble_lock_json): with pytest.raises(NotAuthenticatedError): Lock().unlock()
lock = Lock.from_json(mock_auth, ble_lock_json) lock.unlock()
command_json = { "data": { "CAT": "abcdef", "deviceId": "__ble_uuid__", "state": 0, "userId": "<user-id>", }, "name": "changelockstate", } > mock_auth.request.assert_called_once_with( "post", "devices/__ble_uuid__/commands", json=command_json )

def assert_called_with(self, /, *args, **kwargs): """assert that the last call was made with the specified arguments.

testcase.name: test_add_access_code

failure.message: AssertionError: Calls not found. Expected: [call('post', 'devices/__wifi_uuid__/commands', json={'data': {'friendlyName': 'Friendly name', 'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'notification': 0, 'notificationEnabled': False, 'disabled': 0, 'activationSecs': 0, 'expirationSecs': 4294967295, 'schedule1': {'daysOfWeek': '7F', 'startHour': 0, 'startMinute': 0, 'endHour': 23, 'endMinute': 59}}, 'name': 'addaccesscode'}), call('post', 'notifications/<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', {'notificationId': '<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', 'userId': '<user-id>', 'deviceId': '__wifi_uuid__', 'devicetypeId': 'be489wifi', 'notificationDefinitionId': 'onunlockstateaction', 'filterValue': 'Friendly name', 'active': False})] Actual: [call('POST', 'devices/__wifi_uuid__/commands', body='{"data": {"friendlyName": "Friendly name", "accessCode": 123, "accessCodeLength": 4, "notification": 0, "notificationEnabled": false, "disabled": 0, "activationSecs": 0, "expirationSecs": 4294967295, "schedule1": {"daysOfWeek": "7F", "startHour": 0, "startMinute": 0, "endHour": 23, "endMinute": 59}}, "name": "addaccesscode"}', headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}), call('post', 'notifications/<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', {'notificationId': '<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', 'userId': '<user-id>', 'deviceId': '__wifi_uuid__', 'devicetypeId': 'be489wifi', 'notificationDefinitionId': 'onunlockstateaction', 'active': False, 'filterValue': 'Friendly name'})]

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_lock.TestLock object at 0x107052150> mock_auth = <MagicMock spec_set='Auth' id='4419235728'> lock_json = {'CAT': '01234', 'SAT': '98765', 'attributes': {'CAT': '01234', 'SAT': '98765', 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'actAlarmBuzzerEnabled': 0, ...}, 'connected': True, ...} access_code_json = {'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'accesscodeId': '__access_code_uuid__', 'activationSecs': 0, ...} notification_json = {'active': False, 'deviceId': '__wifi_uuid__', 'devicetypeId': 'be489wifi', 'filterValue': 'Friendly name', ...}
def test_add_access_code( self, mock_auth: Mock, lock_json: dict[str, Any], access_code_json: dict[str, Any], notification_json: dict[str, Any], ): lock = Lock.from_json(mock_auth, lock_json) code = AccessCode.from_json(mock_auth, lock, access_code_json) # Users should not set these. code._auth = None code._device = None code.access_code_id = None code.device_id = None json = code.to_json()
notification_json["active"] = False mock_auth.request.side_effect = [ Mock(json=Mock(return_value=access_code_json)), Mock(json=Mock(return_value=notification_json)), ] lock.add_access_code(code)
del notification_json["createdAt"] del notification_json["updatedAt"] > mock_auth.request.assert_has_calls( [ call( "post", "devices/__wifi_uuid__/commands", json={"data": json, "name": "addaccesscode"}, ), call( "post", "notifications/<user-id>___access_code_uuid__", notification_json, ), ] )
tests/test_lock.py:331: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='mock.request' spec_set='function' id='4419231024'> calls = [call('post', 'devices/__wifi_uuid__/commands', json={'data': {'friendlyName': 'Friendly name', 'accessCode': 123, 'ac...Id': 'be489wifi', 'notificationDefinitionId': 'onunlockstateaction', 'filterValue': 'Friendly name', 'active': False})] any_order = False
def assert_has_calls(self, calls, any_order=False): """assert the mock has been called with the specified calls. The `mock_calls` list is checked for the calls.
If `any_order` is False (the default) then the calls must be sequential. There can be extra calls before or after the specified calls.
If `any_order` is True then the calls can be in any order, but they must all appear in `mock_calls`.""" expected = [self._call_matcher(c) for c in calls] cause = next((e for e in expected if isinstance(e, Exception)), None) all_calls = _CallList(self._call_matcher(c) for c in self.mock_calls) if not any_order: if expected not in all_calls: if cause is None: problem = 'Calls not found.' else: problem = ('Error processing expected calls.\n' 'Errors: {}').format( [e if isinstance(e, Exception) else None for e in expected]) > raise AssertionError( f'{problem}\n' f'Expected: {_CallList(calls)}\n' f' Actual: {safe_repr(self.mock_calls)}' ) from cause E AssertionError: Calls not found. E Expected: [call('post', 'devices/__wifi_uuid__/commands', json={'data': {'friendlyName': 'Friendly name', 'accessCode': 123, 'accessCodeLength': 4, 'notification': 0, 'notificationEnabled': False, 'disabled': 0, 'activationSecs': 0, 'expirationSecs': 4294967295, 'schedule1': {'daysOfWeek': '7F', 'startHour': 0, 'startMinute': 0, 'endHour': 23, 'endMinute': 59}}, 'name': 'addaccesscode'}), E call('post', 'notifications/<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', {'notificationId': '<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', 'userId': '<user-id>', 'deviceId': '__wifi_uuid__', 'devicetypeId': 'be489wifi', 'notificationDefinitionId': 'onunlockstateaction', 'filterValue': 'Friendly name', 'active': False})] E Actual: [call('POST', 'devices/__wifi_uuid__/commands', body='{"data": {"friendlyName": "Friendly name", "accessCode": 123, "accessCodeLength": 4, "notification": 0, "notificationEnabled": false, "disabled": 0, "activationSecs": 0, "expirationSecs": 4294967295, "schedule1": {"daysOfWeek": "7F", "startHour": 0, "startMinute": 0, "endHour": 23, "endMinute": 59}}, "name": "addaccesscode"}', headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}), E call('post', 'notifications/<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', {'notificationId': '<user-id>___access_code_uuid__', 'userId': '<user-id>', 'deviceId': '__wifi_uuid__', 'devicetypeId': 'be489wifi', 'notificationDefinitionId': 'onunlockstateaction', 'active': False, 'filterValue': 'Friendly name'})]
